授業目的: This course is designed to help students learn about and develop their practical, academic and other writing skills in English. Students will be encouraged to take ownership of language learning as a life-long process through various forms of engagement in writing tasks in and outside the classroom. Writing activities will aim at improving students' cultural and intercultural knowledge and awareness. In addition, students will learn to approach their ideas from a global perspective, including thinking about issues critically. The course aims to improve skills that assist students with organizing their ideas and writing, including supporting, comparing, and summarizing information. will complete writing assignments and be given opportunities to self-edit, as well as editing others' work through collaboration. There will be four small writing tasks based on the units covered, and one final writing task.
到達目標: To improve students' fluency of expressing their thoughts and ideas using written English.To improve students' understanding of and engagement with the writing process in English, including: Planning sentences, Prewriting, Writing, and Revising.To improve students' abilities to develop and organize written ideas.To improve students' knowledge of and ability to use various types of grammar in writing.To improve students' cultural and intercultural understanding, awareness, and collaboration through cooperative learning and completing various writing activities.
授業方法: Most course content will be delivered using a process approach to teaching writing. Students will be required to complete activities individually with opportunities for collaboration and cooperative learning. Students will be required to complete between 30 and 60 minutes of homework per lesson, on their own, as assigned by the instructor. This will include online quizzes, and completing writing assignments not finished during class. For students that are not able to attend classes in person, online, on-demand classes with weekly assignments will be an option.

教科書/Required texts0:
There is no required textbook for this course. All materials will be provided by the teacher and will be available on Google Classroom. 
Students are expected to have an A4 lined notebook for use in every class for writing activities.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is placed within the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school. By studying these courses with the DP and CP in mind, you will develop the abilities expected by each faculty and graduate school. The DP, CP, and curriculum schedule (course list, etc.) for each faculty and graduate school are available at the link below; please use them as a reference for your study plan. https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1 *This is a link to kwic (accessible only to current students).

備考Note0: Prerequisite: Taking the TOEIC test required by KGU in December is a prerequisite for earning credit.Absences: Four or more absences are considered excessive and result in automatic failure. Leaving a classroom without asking for permission, doing something other than class activities, or sleeping is considered an absence.Tardiness: Arriving in the classroom after the bell is considered tardiness. Arriving 30 minutes after the bell is considered an absence. Two tardy arrivals equal one absence. (Being late is not considered tardiness in the case of(documented delay of public transportation.) Make-up Assessments: Make-up assessments will not be given except in cases of documented medical necessity or family emergency. Cheating:Cheating in any quizzes, tests, projects,or assignmentsresults in zero points on the work. Please note that leaving the classroom without permission from the teacher, working on other subjects, or sleeping during class will be counted as an absence.
